blocks.body: Possessive forms (possessive forms) show ownership - whose is it. There are two forms in English: possessive adjectives (possessive adjectives) - my, your, his, her, its, our, their, which stand before a noun, and possessive pronouns (possessive pronouns) - mine, yours, his, hers, ours, theirs, which replace a noun.

{ "title": "8. Brief synopsis", "items": [ "Possessive adjectives — before a noun: my, your, his, her, its, our, their.", "Possessive pronouns — instead of a noun: mine, yours, his, hers, ours, theirs.", "Is there a noun after? → Adjective (my book). No noun? → Pronoun (mine) It's = it is.", "His — єдине слово з однаковою формою: his book (adj), it's his (pronoun).", "Its (присвійний прикметник) НЕ має форми pronoun. It's = it is.", "In response to Whose? we use pronouns: mine, yours, his, hers, ours, theirs.", "Pronoun cannot stand with a noun: mine book (✗), my book (✓).", "Adjective cannot stand alone: ​​This is my (✗), This is mine (✓)." ] }
